Notice Of Motion To Stay Deposition And Quash Deposition Subpoena To Rhmt Llc Or Alternatively For Protective Order
Off Calendar Feb-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nina Patane, Esq. 831-417-3611 of Patane Gumberg Avila LLP for Plaintiff Denai Avila through
Court Call.
John Lofton, Esq. 415-957-1800 of Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP for Defendant RHMT, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT RHMT, LLC's Motion To Stay Deposition And Quash Deposition Subpoena To Rhmt Llc Or Alternatively For Protective Order is Granted in part and Denied in part.

RHMT is to produce its PMQ to testify on (1) RHMT's involvement, direct or indirect, if any, in the management of Igzactly Enterprises prior to April 30, 2017, (2) RHMT's involvement, direct or indirect, if any, in the employment of Plaintiffs, (3) RHMT's involvement, direct or indirect, if any, in the termination of Plaintiffs' employment, (3) any communications between RHMT and any other defendant prior to May 19, 2017 regarding any of the Plaintiffs, their employment or the termination of their employment, and (5) RHMT's responsibility, if any, with respect to any liabilities or obligations of any other defendant, pursuant to the Sale Agreement, the Assignment Agreement, the Management Consulting and Staffing Agreement or any other written or oral agreement. Not less than three court days before the deposition, RHMT will produce any documents in its possession, custody or control that (A) relate to RHMT's involvement in the employment of Plaintiffs, (B) relate to RHMT's involvement in the termination of Plaintiffs' employment, (C) reflect any communication between RHMT and any other defendant prior to May 19, 2017 regarding any of the Plaintiffs, their employment or termination. The requests for discovery sanctions are denied.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/JPT/HK)

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. Thefore, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT RHMT, LLC's Motion To Stay Deposition And Quash Deposition Subpoena To RHMT LLC Or Alternatively For Protective Order, as follows:

Given the fact that Defendant RHMT's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint was, on December 14, 2017, sustained with 20 days leave to amend, this discovery matter is continued for at least 45 days. The parties shall jointly select a date for the hearing and confirm the availability for that date with the discovery department.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

[Per JPT Cohen, parties are to contact the discovery clerk re continuance date.]

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Nina M. Patane of the law offices of Patane Gumberg Avila LLP (831-417-3611) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Patrick R. Kitchin of the law offices of Kitchin Legal, APC (415-677-9058) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification as to the language of the proposed order.

DEFENDANT JESSE SMITH's MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to portions of the first amended complaint is: 1) GRANTED without leave to amend as to the request for "consequential damages" on first through fourth, sixth through ninth, and twelfth causes of action; 2) GRANTED without leave to amend as to the request for attorney's fees on first and second causes of action; 3) GRANTED without leave to amend as to the request for civil penalties on the first through fourth and sixth through ninth causes of action; and 4) GRANTED without leave to amend as to the request for "premiums" on the fifth cause of action; and 5) DENIED as to all other matters sought to be stricken. 1) The cited statutes define what damages are recoverable, which does not include consequential damages. Plaintiffs' discussion of Tameny v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (1980) 27 Cal.3d 167 is inapposite as plaintiffs do not allege a claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. 2) Plaintiffs cannot recover fees based on Labor Code sec. 226.7. (Kirby v. Immoos Fire Protection, Inc. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 1244, 1255 (superseded by statute on other grounds).) 3) Plaintiffs can only recover civil penalties on a PAGA claim. (Iskanian v. CLS Transportation, LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348, 381.) 4) Plaintiffs' acknowledge that their request for "premiums" lacks merit. 5) Plaintiffs can recover fees on their twelfth cause of action. The punitive damages allegations and paragraph 88 are adequately alleged. Plaintiffs allege at paragraph 15 that Mr. Smith formulated the illegal corporate policies. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. The order is to include language that striking civil penalties under non-PAGA claims does not preclude the availability of statutory penalties undoer those claims.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com (L&M302)

OFF THE RECORD: Later this same date: Written order is signed and filed.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Nina M. Patane of the law offices of Patane Gumberg Avila LLP (831-417-3611) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Patrick R. Kitchin of the law offices of Kitchin Legal, APC (415-677-9058) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification as to the language of the proposed order.

DEFENDANT RHMT, LLC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all claims alleged against it in the first amended complaint is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to all claims. Plaintiffs have leave to allege facts regarding the information that led them to believe that RHMT assumed Igzactly Enterprises, Inc.'s liabilities and/or that RHMT, LLC acted as a joint employer. (Gomes v. Countrywide Home Loans, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1149, 1158.) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. The order is to include language that striking civil penalties under non-PAGA claims does not preclude the availability of statutory penalties under those claims.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com (L&M302)

OFF THE RECORD: Later this same date: Written order is signed and filed.
